mission.title: keda: Add option for the Datadog scaler to use the Datadog Cluster Agent as source of metrics
mission.description: Add option for the Datadog scaler to use the Datadog Cluster Agent as source of metrics. Requested by 13+ users.

steps.title: Check current keda deployment
steps.description:
Verify your keda version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n keda -l app.kubernetes.io/name=keda
helm list -n keda 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working keda installation.

steps.title: Review keda configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant keda configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n keda -l app.kubernetes.io/name=keda
kubectl get configmap -n keda -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=keda
```
### Proposal

Right now, the Datadog scaler polls the Datadog REST API to get the query values that will drive scaling events.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Add option for the Datadog scaler to use the Datadog…
steps.description:
Add option to the Datadog scaler to use the Datadog Cluster Agent as proxy to obtain the metrics, instead of calling the REST API directly.

This avoids duplication and also improves the rate limiting issue that the Datadog scaler currently has, as the Cluster Agent gets the metric values in batches.

Implements: #5355
- [X] A PR is opened to update the documentation on ([repo](https://github.com/kedacore/keda-docs)) *(if applicable)*
- [X] Commits are signed with Developer Certificate of

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n keda -l app.kubernetes.io/name=keda
kubectl get events -n keda --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Add option for the Datadog scaler to use the Datadog…" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
Add option to the Datadog scaler to use the Datadog Cluster Agent as proxy to obtain the metrics, instead of calling the REST API directly.

This avoids duplication and also improves the rate limiting issue that the Datadog scaler currently has, as the Cluster Agent gets the metric values in batches.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with keda installed or the issue environment reproducible.
